stages.objective: Someone on the docks remembers the clerk who stopped coming to work. Ask around - the Mission pot, the Beaching Strand, the Wrackhouse counter, or the rooming house that still keeps his bed.
stages.log: Dock talk named the counting-house clerk who vanished the night a ledger line was erased: Tam Sedge, lodged four doors from the desk he never came back to.

stages.objective: Ottavan Crell knows which line was erased. Ask him at the Weighhouse.
stages.log: Crell said it plain enough: one line gone from the King's ledger, and the clerk who copied it gone the same night.

stages.objective: Reach the clerk's desk inside the Counting-House. Its door answers to the Watch's colors - wear a face it respects, dash the Watch's grace, or lift Master Gilt's vault key.
stages.log: Got past the Counting-House door, to the desk the clerk left behind.

stages.objective: The clerk's desk is still his. Open it.
stages.log: The locked drawer gave up a torn ledger leaf - the copied line, in the clerk's own hand.

stages.objective: The torn leaf names the night. Crell would raise it. Gilt would bury it. The rooftops would trade on it.
stages.log: The torn leaf found its keeper.

stages.objective: Done. The Harbormaster holds the proof, and the Watch knows who brought it. Master Gilt will not forget the face that raised it.
stages.log: Crell took the leaf to the Weighhouse scales. The erased line stands raised, and the King's banker remembers who raised it.

stages.objective: Done. The banker paid Royals for a buried truth, and the Watch and the Temple both mark the smell of it.
stages.log: Gilt fed the leaf to a candle and counted out forty Royals. The line stays erased; the clerk stays a rumor.

stages.objective: Done. The rooftops keep the receipt now, and the brotherhood remembers who brought it up the wall.
stages.log: Finch folded the leaf away without reading it twice. The rooftops keep receipts, and this one is banked.

stages.objective: The Netter house stands on a pawnbroker's patience. The widow will say it plain if asked - or the ward will: Grandmother Withy sees everything worth a price on the Arcade, and Herdis hears whatever the pens hear.
stages.log: The whisper is real. After the boats were lost, Widow Annis Netter signed paper at Fenner's window against the house itself - and Fenner keeps that paper in the strongbox behind his cage.

stages.objective: Get the widow's paper out of Fenner's strongbox: crack the box behind the cage, put Royals on Fenner's counter until he sells his patience, or wear a face the Merchants' Guild vouches for and watch him fold.
stages.log: The way to the widow's paper opened - Fenner's box, Fenner's price, or Fenner's fear of the Guild.

stages.objective: The deal is struck and the Royals are counted. Fenner has unlocked the back room - take the paper from the strongbox and be gone before he re-counts his reasons.
stages.log: Fenner took the Royals and turned the strongbox key himself. What a pawnbroker sells, he sells.
stages.notes: The thirty repeated give_item effects ARE the purchase price: the engine has no pay-a-party effect (pay moves coins party->owner only), and each give_item moves one Royal owner->fenner, seize-what-exists per coin (the fine/pay precedent - a short pocket pays what it carries). Declared in the S4 WORLD phase report; collapse to a single paid-count effect if sim-core ever grows one.

stages.objective: Fenner wants no quarrel with the Guild face at his window. The cage stands open - take the paper from the strongbox yourself; he will watch, and say nothing worth repeating.
stages.log: Fenner weighed the Guild's regard against the widow's paper and turned the strongbox key without a word.

stages.objective: The Netter house is folded in a coat pocket now. Bring the paper to Widow Annis Netter and end the debt - or back to Fenner's window for his price - or keep it, and let the ward stop asking. Sit on it long enough and that choice makes itself.
stages.log: The widow's paper found its keeping.

stages.objective: Done. The widow holds her own paper and calls it kindling for her own hearth. The fisher-families of the quay know whose hand brought it home - and Fenner will not forget the face that emptied his box.
stages.log: Annis Netter took the paper in both hands and named it kindling. The quay knows whose hand brought it home; the pawnbroker knows it too.

stages.objective: Done. The paper went back behind Fenner's cage and his Royals crossed the counter the other way. The Guild counts you reliable; the quay counts what you sold.
stages.log: Fenner counted out twenty-five Royals without haggling once - the only respect he pays. The widow's house stands on his patience again, and the quay knows who resold it.

stages.objective: Done. The paper sleeps in a coat pocket. The Netter house stands on YOUR patience now, and Fenner's window waits on a price that never comes. The rooftops call that banking.
stages.log: Nobody got the widow's paper. The ward stopped asking; the coat pocket did not. Leverage held is leverage earning - the roofline's own arithmetic.

notes: S3 'The Vanished Clerk' - the game's first authored quest. Fiction seed: the gilt-crell-erasure micro-history (histories.json). The clerk never spawns (roster stays 692): he is the locked desk, the torn leaf, and the people who remember him - canon name Tam Sedge (DOCKS-GAZETTEER 4.6). World S3 pass folded in the Sim phase's declared cut: the 'sedge' landlady party + her rumor trigger (both lists append-only) and the prose polish. Quest talk tables live in barks.json under quest.vanished-clerk.* (key vocabulary linted by DocksBarkContentTest against THIS file's stages x parties). The pay effect seizes-what-exists; the bake tops Gilt's pocket to cover it. --- S4 adds 'The Widow's Paper' (quest #2, appended so the vanished-clerk keeps entry 0): fiction seed netter-fenner-debt (histories.json), a judgment/leverage quest proving the engine is a general machine - ZERO engine edits (one append-only ItemKinds.DEBT_PAPER constant plus bake bindings; 'royals' binds to the COIN kind). Three acquisition routes (crack the strongbox / pay Fenner / merchants-standing yield) into three endings (widow / Fenner / keep). The keep ending is after_ticks BY DESIGN: sitting on the paper IS the choice, and the journal objective says so out loud. Props staged at bake: 1 DEBT_PAPER on the K15 strongbox cell, Fenner's pocket topped to fund end_fenner's seize-what-exists pay (Gilt precedent).
